items.resolution: LANDED on integration/next (bundle PR #146) 2026-07-17 @c338254, NOTICEABLE. Owner ask verbatim: 'Can you make the target draggable? The one on the yardage book and map?' MAP surface already shipped v1.1.10 (bundle #143, map-fieldtest-v119 item 4) -- verify-only, untouched this cycle. BOOK surface (NEW): draggable ink ring+crosshair reticle added to HoleIllustration.tsx (the on-paper SVG schematic). Fable plan specs/draggable-target-plan.md + designer concept specs/yardage-target-concept.md. Geometry (new pure module frontend/src/lib/yardage-book-target.ts +16 tests): ypu = hole.yards/arcLength(path); toTarget=round5(|P-tee|*ypu), toGreen=round5(|P-green|*ypu); free 2D drag clamped to paper; origin=tee (book is abstract/non-georeferenced, no GPS -- coherent-magnitude not byte-identical to the map's real-coord numbers, documented as the achievable bar). Pointer Events + setPointerCapture + getScreenCTM().inverse(); >=44px hit target; touch-action:none scoped to the hit circle; drag isolated from HoleCard expand/zoom. Readout = HoleCard's EXISTING top-right ink pill grown to two lines (FROM TEE/TO GREEN) while a custom aim is active, collapsing to default ###Y otherwise; DOM x to clear; single dashed target->green line only (calmer than the map, per concept); reduced-motion respected. REVIEW ARC: reviewer SHIP (math/pointer-lifecycle/render-loop safe, map untouched, security nil); qa PASS (lint/tsc/build/voice-278/unit-16/map+yardage-349 + live Playwright drive); designer BLOCKED twice then PASS -- (1) fixed a double-pill + dogleg green/flag collision by lifting the readout to HoleCard's single pill (deleted the in-SVG panel), (2) caught via LIVE render that a capture-phase framer-isolation hack (co-located onPointerDownCapture{stopPropagation}+onPointerDown on the SAME node) SILENTLY KILLED the drag in React 19 (dispatch-abort) -> reverted @c338254 to the QA-verified bubble onPointerDown+setPointerCapture mechanism, builder LIVE-re-verified all 5 behaviors. Commits b142f40(build) + 997e318(pill lift) + c338254(React-19 revert) on integration/next. NON-BLOCKING follow-up filed: yardage-book-card-wobble-drag-isolation. items.title: Yardage-book card rubber-bands sideways during an aim-target drag (framer drag='x' isolation)
items.resolution: FIXED on integration/next (bundle #146, silent rider, not noticeable), 2026-07-17. Chose candidate (a) from the diagnosis: wired the reticle's pointerdown/move/up/cancel via NATIVE addEventListener (ref+useEffect, empty deps, latest-closure via a toSvgPointRef mirror) directly on the hit-target <circle> in HoleIllustration.tsx, replacing the React onPointerDown/Move/Up JSX props. Native listeners fire at the true DOM target phase, so calling stopPropagation() there runs BEFORE the event ever reaches framer's ancestor drag='x' wrapper (which installs its own native pointerdown listener) -- unlike React's SyntheticEvent.stopPropagation(), which only runs after React's root-delegated dispatch, i.e. too late (that was the root cause). Did NOT reintroduce the rejected co-located onPointerDownCapture+onPointerDown pattern -- this is a single native listener set, no React capture/bubble handlers on this node at all. Rejected (b) [flip drag=false on ancestor] as a bigger, riskier prop-lift across 3 components for a race-prone fix; rejected (c) [intermediate capture-div guard] as unnecessary once (a) fully isolates at the source. LIVE-RENDER VERIFIED via a throwaway Playwright-driven harness page (deleted before commit, not part of the diff) that reproduced the EXACT ancestor drag='x' wrapper from RoundPageClient's mock/no-course branch around HoleCard: (1) confirmed the harness reproduces the bug pre-fix (wrapper's computed transform visibly translated x 1px->10px during a 10-step reticle drag, matrix() not 'none' after release) -- proves the test is meaningful; (2) post-fix, wrapper transform stayed 'none' throughout an identical reticle drag while the reticle itself moved (FROM TEE/TO GREEN readout appeared, values changed) -- card rock still, drag fully functional; (3) off-reticle small swipe still advances holes (draggedRef gate untouched); (4) fast 300px flick still advances holes. Gates: lint clean, tsc clean, voice-tests 278/278, targeted vitest 409/409 (yardage-book-target 16 + DistancesCard/Transcript + all map/course suites). Only frontend/src/components/yardage/HoleIllustration.tsx changed (removed the now-unused `PointerEvent as ReactPointerEvent` import too). No backend/db changes.
items.why: Designer PASS finding on yardage-book-draggable-aim-target (bundle #146, 2026-07-17, verified LIVE): while dragging the reticle on HoleCard, the ancestor framer-motion <motion.div drag='x'> (RoundPageClient.tsx ~L2211, mock/no-course fallback branch) still visually rubber-bands the whole card a few dozen px and snaps back on release. It NEVER commits a hole change or opens zoom (verified small/fast/300px flick -- criterion d held), but the wobble reads jittery/buggy, not calm (NORTHSTAR) on the feature's primary gesture, and is self-limiting only by framer's commit threshold (fragile). ROOT CAUSE: HoleIllustration.handlePointerDown calls React-synthetic e.stopPropagation() but framer's drag PanSession reacts to NATIVE pointer bubbling, which React-synthetic stopPropagation does not stop; handlePointerMove/endDrag don't stop it either. LEARNED THIS CYCLE (do NOT repeat): an onPointerDownCapture{stopPropagation} co-located with the bubble onPointerDown on the SAME node ABORTS React 19's dispatch and kills the drag entirely. Correct directions (each needs a LIVE pointer-drive re-verify, not a code read): (a) drive the reticle drag via NATIVE addEventListener on the hit element (ref+useEffect) with native stopPropagation on pointerdown/move so framer's ancestor native listener never sees it -- but the drag logic must then live in the native handler; (b) flip the motion.div drag prop to false while a reticle drag is active (lift the existing onAimChange dragging signal up to RoundPageClient, reuse the draggedRef pattern); (c) intermediate-div onPointerDownCapture guard like the map branch at RoundPageClient.tsx:1983 (only where the descendant doesn't need React-synthetic pointerdown -> pair with (a)). Fix before bundle #146 reaches TestFlight so the owner's first drag feels calm.

items.title: Ask-caddie orb sometimes stalls at Connecting and never reaches Listening (owner P0, Bug A)
items.resolution: LANDED on integration/next (bundle PR #145) 2026-07-17 @fbd2061, NOTICEABLE. Owner v1.1.11 field P0. ROOT CAUSE: useCaddieLiveSession armed ONE 3s deadline over the whole connect (mint + WebRTC ICE + SDP), cleared only on pc.onconnectionstatechange==='connected'; 3s is too tight for mint+ICE on a course cell, and on timeout it fell back to classic -- while the sheet is CLOSED (pill idle-tap path) useDetachedCaddieLive then auto-released liveOn so the pill silently reverted to 'Ask caddie' (no retry, no honest failure = the owner's 'doesn't go to listening'). FIX (fable plan specs/caddie-live-p0-connect-hole-plan.md): per-phase budgets LIVE_MINT_BUDGET_MS=4000 (via the onMinted seam) + LIVE_CONNECT_BUDGET_MS=8000 (ICE) in transport.ts; ONE quiet cold auto-retry (failPreConnect, retryUsedRef-bounded, old client setEvents({})+stop() before the new one); new honest states liveState 'retrying' ('Still connecting…') and 'connect-failed' ('Couldn't connect — tap to retry') that PERSIST liveOn (connect-failed != fallback so useDetachedCaddieLive's fallback-auto-release is untouched), mic torn down, tap-retries/hold-ends; Slice-D reconnect/resume deadlines widened 3s->8s; key-free voiceEvent('caddie','live_connect_*') phase telemetry; realtime.ts untouched for A. Mic-deny classification made UNCONDITIONAL (deviation, reviewer verified load-bearing). Reviewer SHIP (retry can't zombie, late events inert, mic-deny -> immediate honest fallback never a spinner, connect-failed tears down mic + has escapes, guards intact). qa PASS (tsc/lint/voice-278/vitest-2669/caddie-experience-271/build/ruff). designer APPROVE (label shortened to avoid 320px truncation; states calm, no SaaS-toast drift, pill never falsely pulses). New test useCaddieLiveSession.connect.test.tsx (11 cases). NON-BLOCKING follow-up: cold mic-deny constructs one throwaway retry client (wasted mint + phantom live_connect_retry breadcrumb) before fallback wins -- terminal state correct, rare once-per-user. Ships HELD pending tree-distance verification lane; rides the eventual verified ship on bundle #145.

items.title: Live caddie answered about hole 1 while the golfer was looking at hole 2 (owner P0, Bug B)
items.resolution: LANDED on integration/next (bundle PR #145) 2026-07-17 @fbd2061 (same commit as Bug A), NOTICEABLE. Owner v1.1.11 field P0. ROOT CAUSE: the live session re-anchored the model on hole swipe via anchorHole()->sendContext(buildHoleContextText) (a role:system STEER only), but dispatchTool (realtime.ts) still resolved the hole from the model's stale args.hole_number, and the backend defaulted an omitted hole to the mint-time session.current_hole (never updated on swipe) -- so a lagging model answered the old hole, incoherent with the client's live hole (same class as the shipped '125' yardage-incoherence fix which already made get_recommendation carry the client's resolved yards/basis via getToolContext). FIX (structural, fable plan): getToolContext (useCaddieLiveSession) now carries the LIVE currentHole as a single snapshot off holeContextRef, read at dispatch/ANSWER time; the six hole-scoped tools (get_recommendation/get_conditions/get_carries/get_bend/get_shot_distance/get_green_read) resolve ctx-first (liveHole ?? args) overriding stale args.hole_number -- structurally cannot go stale; record_shot stays args-first (a shot names its own hole). No backend / shared-types change; useVoiceCaddie (sets no tool context) byte-identical. Reviewer SHIP (single-read triple can't mix across a swipe, no 0/NaN/null hole leak, override coherent with the yardage fix). qa PASS. Extended realtime-dispatch.test.ts (Bug B pins, all six tools + record_shot both ways + ctx-absent byte-identical) + useDetachedCaddieLive.test.tsx (hole-swipe-mid-session). OWNER-DECISION flagged in plan: other-hole voice questions now answer the on-screen hole (recourse: swipe); default is unconditional override. Ships HELD pending tree-distance verification lane; rides bundle #145.

items.title: Course discovery: Augusta-styled descriptions + honest stars/stats + map slide-up detail card
items.why: OWNER DIRECTIVE (2026-07-17, 2 screenshots — a bare Bethpage Black detail page, a map pin with only a marker): 'improve the search experience and course finding significantly. Each course seeded with an Augusta-styled description + a collection of user-reviewed stars and stats if available. The map view should show a slide-up modal with course details, not just the marker.' Spec: specs/course-discovery-intel.md. THREE builds, one PR: (1) Augusta-broadcast-register per-course description, LLM-WRITTEN by a new backend/app/caddie/course_intel_writer.py mirroring guide_writer.py's grounded-writer + fail-closed-validator pattern — an always-present 'landscape' paragraph grounded ONLY in our own geometry (par/yardage/holes/hazard density via existing hazards.extract_hole_hazards), PLUS confidence-gated factual claims (architect/year/style/notable history) from the model's own knowledge (no web_search — cheaper, no injection surface) that the validator DROPS unless self-reported confidence == 'high' (wrong architect worse than none, [[no-fake-data-fallbacks]]); cached per course, precomputed offline at mapping time (never inline on a request); seed Bethpage Black+Red+Pebble now via a one-off backfill. STORAGE FORK flagged for the fable plan to finalize: Option A (recommended) = new additive Alembic migration adding courses.course_intel jsonb on public.courses (correct cardinality, no coupling to hole/feature churn, but routes through the standing migration->owner-approval gate); Option B = reuse hole_features.properties JSONB on a hole's green feature (the strategy_guide precedent, no migration, but semantically muddy + fragile to re-mapping). (2) Stars+stats: new GET /api/courses/{id}/intel -> CourseIntel (Pydantic in backend/app/models.py, TS in frontend/src/lib/types.ts) surfacing course_reviews avg+count (reuses the EXACT course_key=courses.id identity CourseDetailClient.tsx:64 already passes — no new bridging needed; honestly the OWNER's own reviews only, multi-user star aggregation deferred to B5) + real stats (parTotal/yardageByTee/holesMapped from existing holes/tee_sets tables, roundsPlayed = new COUNT query on Round.mapped_course_id, avgScore = null unless >=1 COMPLETE round exists via Score rows — never averaged over partial rounds). External/Google ratings explicitly OUT (budget doctrine, note as a future config-flip option, rough ~$5-17/1000 Places lookups, key already live per 0d2f535). (3) Map slide-up detail card: DELETES the thin one-row 'Tap card' bar in frontend/src/components/CourseScoutMap.tsx:402-481, replaced by a slide-up sheet (study CaddiePanel.tsx's existing sheet mechanics as the idiom — no new sheet library) rendering the SAME CourseIntel shape as the detail page (name, description first-lines+expand, stars+count, key stats, Add/Start a round/View course actions) fetched on-tap via the SAME /intel endpoint, DB-only, zero external calls. Honest empty states specified for every field (no reviews/not mapped/no description yet/no complete rounds). Budget invariants: no new Places/GolfAPI calls anywhere; description LLM spend is precompute-only (one Claude call per course, no web_search tool); /intel and the map-card tap read our own Postgres only. NOTICEABLE (all three ship as one bundle — new prose + real stars/stats + a materially different map-tap experience, all visible on TestFlight). NEEDS: fable plan to pick the storage fork + finalize field-level detail, builder, adversarial reviewer, qa (verify zero external network calls fire on map-tap + detail-page load), designer (yardage-book sheet feel, NORTHSTAR-gated).

PROD STOP items (apply migration 015 to prod + run 3-course seed Bethpage Black/Red + Pebble) are BLOCKED PENDING DIRECT OWNER AUTHORIZATION: the permission-system classifier blocked the on-box SSM prod write because a cross-session coordinator message is not the owner's own consent for a production deploy. items.title: context_hazards_match only matches the first run of a split trees hazard line
items.why: Non-blocking finding from the caddie-tree-span-gap review (2026-07-17, landed @6feb8a4). tests/eval/checks.py::context_hazards_match was changed from re.search to finditer intending to cover the new gap-split hazard lines (e.g. 'trees L 30-65y and 265-480y'), but the regex '\btrees\s+L\s+(\d+)(?:-(\d+))?y\b' only matches the FIRST run — the second run '265-480y' has no 'trees L' prefix, so an expected carry that only the second run covers still false-fails. TEST-HARNESS ONLY, no production impact, and no current golden scenario produces a split (per-side deltas <=60y). FIX: match 'trees SIDE' once, then scan all '\d+(?:-\d+)?y' segments up to the next side letter / comma, accept if any range covers the expected carry +-15y. Do when a golden scenario first needs a split line.

items.title: Smart get_strategy tool: route the voice orb's strategy synthesis through Sonnet 5 server-side (the ChatGPT-parity win)
items.why: OWNER CRUX (2026-07-17): 'ChatGPT 5.6 caddies better from a screenshot. What model is the caddie using?' AUDIT ANSWER: the voice orb he talks to is OpenAI gpt-realtime (a fast SPEECH model, weak reasoning) reading deterministic engine numbers off tools; the strategy SYNTHESIS is done by gpt-realtime itself — that's where it caddies worse than a frontier text model. FIX (the big lever, lever b in the audit): add a realtime-only tool get_strategy that dispatches SERVER-SIDE to a strong reasoning model (Sonnet 5). Design (fable-verified, specs to be written from this): new endpoint POST /caddie/session/strategy {round_id, hole_number} in backend/app/routes/caddie.py; server assembles the FULL grounded payload from EXISTING helpers in app/caddie/tools.py (recommend_payload one-solve numbers + tee_shot_numbers, conditions_payload weather/plays-like/hazards/green-slope, carries_payload, bend_payload, green_read_payload, player_profile_payload) + format_guide_line(intel.strategy_guide) (the Sonnet-5-written guide already in session intel); ONE claude-sonnet-5 call (own env CADDIE_STRATEGY_MODEL defaulting like GUIDE_WRITER_MODEL), NO tools, thinking={type:disabled} (accepted on Sonnet 5; NOT Fable), max_tokens~=300, system reusing the existing grounding rules (speak engine numbers verbatim, never invent hazards); returns {strategy:'~80-word tee-to-green narrative', numbers:{...engine echoes...}}. The realtime model just SPEAKS it. Tool exposure: add REALTIME_ONLY_TOOLS in tools.py appended by realtime_tools(); do NOT add to TEXT_TOOLS (the text mouth is already Claude — a nested LLM tool there is circular and blows the 6s _TOOL_RESOLVE_TIMEOUT_S). This intentionally amends tests/eval/test_tool_parity.py from 'sets equal' to 'shared subset schema-equal + realtime extras enumerated' — a legitimate contract change in the same PR. Frontend: one case 'get_strategy' in dispatchTool (frontend/src/lib/voice/realtime.ts) + one fn in frontend/src/lib/caddie/api.ts, following the get_recommendation pattern (incl. toolContextProvider yardage ride-along). LATENCY: tool round-trip ~1.5-3s (one pre-assembled Sonnet-5 call, thinking disabled/effort-low, ~150 tok out); the live voice covers the gap naturally; cache the synthesized strategy on the session keyed by (hole, payload-hash) so re-asks are instant. COST ~=$0.005/invocation intro pricing. This gives frontier reasoning behind the live voice WITHOUT giving up speech latency. Smallest viable version: endpoint + one realtime-only tool + one dispatch case + parity-test amendment. Prereq/enabler already landed: CADDIE_ADVICE_MODEL + conditional-temperature decoupling (proves the dedicated-env + temp-gating pattern this reuses). NEEDS: fable plan -> spec, builder, reviewer (grounding-contract pins survive), qa (tool-parity amendment + a latency measurement), and a live get_strategy eval before shipping noticeable.
items.resolution: LANDED on bundle #145 2026-07-17 (integration/next @4bbe0e3, NOTICEABLE). BRAIN FLIPPED from the planned Sonnet 5 to OpenAI gpt-5.6-sol per OWNER DIRECTIVE ('send it to ChatGPT 5.5/5.6' — the '5.6 Sol' he benchmarked). Realtime-only get_strategy tool → POST /caddie/session/strategy assembles the full grounded engine payload (recommend/conditions/carries/bend/green-read/player + format_guide_line), calls gpt-5.6-sol via the Responses API over raw httpx (reasoning effort low, max_output_tokens 1024, NO temperature, env CADDIE_STRATEGY_MODEL default gpt-5.6-sol, same OPENAI_API_KEY as realtime — no new dep); fail-closed validator (reuses guide_writer hazard-type + _has_side_flip + the now-shared module-level GUIDE_INJECTION_PATTERN) → deterministic engine-numbers degrade on any reject/error/missing-key/404/timeout, never fabricate, never crash the live turn; response cached per sha256(ground_truth+model). test_tool_parity amended to shared-subset + enumerated-extras {get_strategy}; frontend dispatchTool case + sessionStrategy() api fn; STRATEGY_TOOL_RULE routes strategy/planning/comparison Qs to the tool (spoken verbatim), simple lookups stay on the fast engine tools. Reviewer SHIP (all 4 blocking Qs pass; /security-review clean; /code-review regex-defork nit closed), QA PASS (ruff; tests/eval 155 incl. 26 strategy unit/route tests + Red-1 side-flip REJECT + degraded-line + cache + no-sampling-params pins; guide/strategy/hazard/parity 354/1 on the rebased tree; frontend lint/tsc/voice278-0/vitest2640/build). LATENCY = GATED-LIVE, NOT run in the loop env (no OPENAI key; auto-mode guard blocks prod-secret access) → runs at PRE-SHIP verification via tests/eval/run_strategy_latency.py (target p50<=~2s; fallbacks gpt-5.6-terra/luna or effort:none). Exact gpt-5.6-sol snapshot pin needs an owner-approved on-box /v1/models probe. Text-path parity assessed + DECLINED (nested LLM is circular + blows the 6s _TOOL_RESOLVE_TIMEOUT_S + busts the text prompt-cache; the text lever is caddie-advice-sonnet5-flip). Fable-authored plan: specs/caddie-smart-strategy-tool-plan.md. NEXT: caddie-vision-visual-read (c) sequences AFTER this (feeds this tool's payload); caddie-advice-sonnet5-flip (a) still gated on a live before/after eval.

items.title: Omnipotent caddie intent router: structurally-enforced advice→brain / facts→engine / scores→direct entry on the one live voice session
items.why: OWNER CRUX (2026-07-17, 10th recurrence of wrong-side advice): 'if it is EASILY answerable by notes we can use a weaker model, but in general the brain should be a more advanced model with context of my yardages/tendencies, known course context, and the map data itself. That routing is the crux.' + extension: 'the caddie component is ALL encompassing — depending on what I am saying it should route to different parts of my backend.' Root cause: the weak realtime speech model composed advice from a grab-bag (live payload + POISONED cached guides + its own judgment) because the strategy ingredients were BAKED into its session context. Builds ON the already-landed get_strategy seam (caddie-smart-strategy-tool @4bbe0e3).
items.resolution: LANDED on bundle #146 2026-07-17 (integration/next range ee1efdd..db7c1e6, NOTICEABLE). Two-tier routing made STRUCTURAL + generalized to a full intent router. (1) CONTEXT STRIP: hazard-side detail + cached guide text + bend + green-slope removed from both mouths' baked context (voice_prompts._situation_block + the text prompt) — the weak realtime model has nothing to freelance a side from; tee-numbers/aim/miss/par-sanity kept (they ARE the engine verdict). (2) ROUTING ENFORCED: new pure backend/app/caddie/routing.py::classify_intent (ADVICE/FACT/SCORE/OTHER, list-extensible); realtime enforcement = the strip + strengthened STRATEGY_TOOL_RULE; text/classic path intercepts ADVICE server-side BEFORE the Claude loop → new strategy_turn.run_strategy_turn = the SAME brain (get_strategy → CADDIE_STRATEGY_MODEL gpt-5.6-sol), same cache, same validator (ONE brain, every mouth). (3) READ-TIME GUIDE GATE: new backend/app/caddie/verdict.py — the cached guide is demoted to labeled 'prior notes — may be stale' and included only if its favor-side agrees with the live engine verdict; disagree → dropped + logged key-free (the Red-1 poison class). (4) VERDICT-PINNED validator (favor-side + reachability + recommended-club) → fail-closed to the engine's own line. (5) SCORE = pure routing layer: live session recognizes 'put me down for a 5' / 'par for me, double for Mike' → EXISTING voice parser + EXISTING scorecard write path (record_scores realtime-only tool + lib/caddie/score-entry.ts), direct write + light ack, NO confirm ceremony (owner directive; never-silent applies to passive tracking only). (6) live-GPS fix (brain now fed GPS-to-green) + spoken thinking-bridge. ACCEPTANCE: Red-1 2/2 (poisoned guide present → favor RIGHT/center never LEFT), routing matrix 35/35 (incl. 'what do I need to shoot par on the back nine'=FACT-not-score, 'I had 3 putts'=not-score), structural strip 6/6. Reviewer BLOCKING×2 (over-broad validator regexes silently degrading good advice) fixed + pass-on-good regressions; delta re-review caught a B2 aim-verb leak → hardened to bare 'at the pin' + narrowed putts guard. QA PASS (ruff, backend 2788 offline pytest, tsc, build, vitest 2700/2700, voice 278/278). Designer APPROVE. Fable plan specs/caddie-two-tier-routing-plan.md. Ships HELD (bundle #146 release on owner approval). LATENCY p50 (payload grew) = gated-live, runs at PRE-SHIP via run_strategy_latency.py per the standing evidence bar.

items.title: Flip the text-advice caddie mouths from Sonnet 4.5 to Sonnet 5 (gated on a live before/after eval)
items.why: Lever (a) from the 2026-07-17 caddie model audit. The text advice mouths (/caddie/session/voice + stream, /voice + stream) run claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 via tool_loop.py. The enabling change LANDED this cycle (caddie-advice-model-decoupling): dedicated CADDIE_ADVICE_MODEL env + conditional temperature, so flipping to Sonnet 5 is now a ONE-ENV change (CADDIE_ADVICE_MODEL=claude-sonnet-5) without the 400 the shared ANTHROPIC_MODEL would have caused and without touching memory/OCR/voice. WHAT REMAINS before the flip ships: (1) a LIVE before/after eval — the consistency probe (tests/eval/run_consistency.py) + the tier2 conversation goldens (tests/eval/run_tier2.py) run on both sonnet-4-5 and sonnet-5 candidates, with measured pass counts (needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + bounded spend; NOT available in the eng-lead worktree — run in CI or a keyed env). HONESTY RULE: no flip without measured before/after. (2) update the eval runners: run_consistency.py + run_tier2.py hard-code candidate temperature=0.7 and default the candidate to ANTHROPIC_MODEL — add the same conditional-temperature gate + a CADDIE_ADVICE_MODEL default before evaluating a Sonnet-5 candidate. (3) consider thinking={type:disabled} on the sonnet-5 branch in tool_loop.py (Sonnet 5 runs adaptive thinking by default; max_tokens=300 is a TOTAL cap so thinking could truncate the spoken reply) — the decoupling builder was asked to note this; confirm whether it was added or is still a flip-day rider. NOTE the owner's crux is the VOICE orb (gpt-realtime), not this text path — so caddie-smart-strategy-tool is the higher-impact lever; this flip is a modest, cheap-during-intro ($2/$10 vs $3/$15) quality bump worth doing once proven. Intro pricing window ends 2026-08-31.

items.title: Per-hole satellite 'visual read' — vision-model grounding at hole-open (feasibility spike, then build)
items.why: Lever (c) from the 2026-07-17 caddie model audit — the owner's screenshot insight: the caddie has never SEEN the hole, it only gets polygons, so it misses things like 'trees left' that OSM ingest lacks. DESIGN (spike-then-build): at guide-precompute time (the same job as guide_writer.py) fetch a static satellite tile for the hole's bbox (centerline/tee/green already mapped in PostGIS — overlay them as markers), send to Sonnet 5 vision (high-res ~2576px; ~1.6-4.8K image tokens) together with build_ground_truth_block, ask for a QUALITATIVE visual read only: tree lines left/right, visual fairway width, bail-out side, unmapped waste/bunker shapes. HARD RULE: no yardages — numbers stay with the engine; validate/reject like validate_guide does for text guides. Store as visual_read next to strategy_guide on hole intel; fold into the caddie-smart-strategy-tool payload (and optionally the prompt guide line). COST ~=$0.01-0.02/hole intro pricing, one-time per course (<= ~$0.40/course). LATENCY: none live (precompute + cache like guides); ~3-5s on-demand fallback at a hole-open cache miss. WHAT IT CATCHES: the owner's exact 'trees left' case on holes where OSM has no tree data (partial tree ingest exists — test_tree_hazards.py — so first cross-check coverage). SPIKE OPEN QUESTIONS (do before building): tile source + LICENSING (Mapbox Static vs Google Static), imagery recency, server-side marker-overlay rendering, hallucination rate on low-res tiles. Sequence AFTER caddie-smart-strategy-tool (it feeds that tool's payload).

items.title: OWNER field-test v1.1.9: upside-down bunker letters + missing big bunkers (Red-9) + other-hole tee markers + draggable aim reticle + Red-11 wrong par
items.why: Owner v1.1.9 field-test (3 screenshots, Bethpage Red holes 8/9/11) + hole-4 ask: (1) A/B bunker badge letters render upside-down on holes whose down-the-line camera bearing points south; (2) hole 9's giant sand/waste complex left of the fairway is unmarked (only 2 small bunkers show); (3) holes 8/11 show OTHER holes' tee markers + stray hole-line fragments; (4) make the tap-to-aim target draggable; (5) hole 11 header reads absurd 'PAR 3 · 462Y'. Fable plan: specs/map-fieldtest-v119-plan.md.
items.resolution: DONE on bundle #143 2026-07-16 (landed on integration/next AFTER multi-user P0 slice-2; adds to PR #143 as NOTICEABLE — first noticeable item in that bundle). Fable plan + builder + adversarial reviewer(+/code-review) + qa + designer(BLOCKING) all green. ROOT CAUSES + FIXES: (1) isFlat:true on the lettered bunker badges (GoogleSatelliteMap.tsx) flat-to-ground rotated the north-up letter PNG with the down-the-line bearing -> set isFlat:false (billboard, upright to screen == correct down-the-line); tee dot flipped too for one convention; extracted pure buildBunkerMarkers() seam (frontend/src/lib/map/marker-options.ts) with an isFlat===false assertion test. (2) TWO causes bound (confirmed by a LIVE Overpass probe on Red-9's bbox): a real relation[golf=bunker] multipolygon (id 19545022) the old way-only ingest query never fetched, AND the CORRIDOR_MAX_LATERAL_YARDS=45 + BUNKER_CAP=4 windows. Fix A: osm.py query+parser now ingest relation[golf=bunker] + way/relation[natural=sand] (new _parse_relation_to_multipolygon mirroring the boundary-relation parser) into the bunkers bucket; both consumers accept MultiPolygon (frontend fairwayBunkerCarries yields ONE honest chip per complex with union front/back; backend hazards.py _feature_point centroid) — kept in sync, extract_corridor_profile windows deliberately NOT polluted. Fix B: BUNKER_CAP 4->6 (assets A-F already exist) + fairway-adjacency lateral admit (edge-of-fairway bunkers beyond 45y admitted only into leftover capped slots via smallest-|lateral| ordering, cannot spam/cross-hole). (3) stray other-hole tee markers = a two-writer race on holeMarkerIdsRef (the GPS tick ran an un-serialized clearHoleOverlays->addHoleOverlays outside the camera queue, orphaning a marker) -> routed the GPS-tick overlay refresh through the SAME camera queue via a discriminated {hd,reason:'hole'|'gps',pos} payload (single writer); reviewer caught a follow-on regression (plain last-write-wins let a 'gps' request evict a pending 'hole' request, dropping the reframe+tee-shot redraw) -> made createCameraQueue coalescing PRIORITY-AWARE (shouldReplace predicate; gps never evicts a pending hole). (4) draggable reticle: one shared placeTarget(pos) seam for tap AND drag-end (no math fork), draggable:true + drag listeners guarded to tapMarkerIdRef, live numbers-only on drag ticks + polylines settle on drag-end, light haptic on drag-start. (5) Red-11 is par 4 in OSM (fixture-proven) but stale par 3 in the stored DB -> shipped a display-side sanity guard displayPar(par,yards) (frontend/src/lib/hole/par-sanity.ts, threshold 280 in lockstep with backend PAR_SANITY_MIN_YARDS_FOR_PAR3) so the header renders 'Par —' not a false '3'; the PRIMARY data fix (sanctioned Red RE-INGEST on staging to write par=4, owner's standing approval) is a FOLLOW-UP ops action (filed map-fieldtest-red11-reingest). GATES on the integrated tree (integration/next + these 5 items): tsc clean, eslint clean, next build OK, voice-smoke 278/278, vitest map+hole 300/300 (full suite 2561+ earlier), backend ruff clean, backend pytest 2571 pass/121 DB-skip (CI runs DB tests). Commits 7ef6f2b(item1) 26d76b9(item3) d89d647(item4) ea522f4(item5) 47f6595(item2) fcef3e1(queue fix) on integration/next; spec 855b876. FOLLOW-UPS filed: map-fieldtest-red11-reingest (staging data), map-reticle-grab-affordance (designer non-blocking polish: subtle icon-scale bump on drag-start). No security-review required (no auth/endpoint; osm.py query is a fixed template with numeric-bbox interpolation, no injection surface — /code-review clean).

items.title: Spatial join DROPS relation-multipolygon bunkers (Red-9 waste complex fetched but assembled away)
items.why: Follow-up from tonight's guide-safe prod re-ingest (2026-07-16). The map-fieldtest-v119 Fix A extended the fetch (osm.py query relation[golf=bunker] + way/relation[natural=sand]) and the parser (_parse_relation_to_multipolygon -> bunkers bucket, MultiPolygon geometry) and the consumers (tee-shot-overlays.ts, hazards.py accept MultiPolygon) — but NOT the spatial-join assembly. The re-ingest FETCHED the Red-9 waste complex (relation[golf=bunker] id 19545022; 732 polygon features vs ~700) yet per-hole bunker counts came out byte-identical: the assembly DROPS it. EXACT DROP POINT: backend/app/services/course_spatial.py assign_features_to_holes geometry-type dispatch else-branch (~L412-415) — a MultiPolygon falls through Point/Polygon and is set (None,None,inf), then build_course_feature_collection (~L615) filters it out because course_name is None. FIX: add a MultiPolygon branch — pick the largest member ring (shoelace) as the representative outer_ring, centroid via _ring_centroid, then reuse the existing Tier 1/2/3 assignment + bunker corridor cap (150m) UNCHANGED, so the complex lands on hole 9 like a way-bunker. natural=sand ways already flow through the Polygon path. SILENT (ingest correctness); the visible payoff needs the data re-run (red9-relation-bunker-rerun).
items.resolution: DONE on bundle #143 2026-07-16 (SILENT; rides PR #143 -> merges with the next noticeable ship). Fable plan specs/red9-relation-bunker-assembly-plan.md; builder @89769b1; fresh reviewer SHIP; qa PASS. FIX (backend/app/services/course_spatial.py): new module-level _ring_area(ring) (absolute planar shoelace, NO lat-scaling — used only to RANK member polygons of one complex at a shared latitude; docstring says so) + a new `elif geom_type == "MultiPolygon":` branch in assign_features_to_holes between the Polygon and else branches: iterate members, pick the usable member (non-empty outer ring, >=4 pts) with the largest _ring_area as the representative outer_ring, set clon/clat via _ring_centroid, then FALL THROUGH into the existing Tier 1 (centerline-through-polygon overlap) / Tier 2 (ring-vertex voting) / Tier 3 (centroid-nearest-line) UNCHANGED; no usable member -> keeps the existing (None,None,inf) drop. Doc-only touch-ups (polygons docstring 'Polygon or MultiPolygon'; Tier-1 header comment). Point/Polygon paths byte-identical; build_course_feature_collection woods/rough size filter never sees bunkers (verified); assemble path (osm_ingest flattens bunkers -> poly_by_id keys on relation/<id> osm_id -> geometry copied verbatim) flows the MultiPolygon to storage intact — no change needed. TESTS (backend/tests/test_course_spatial.py): +_make_multipolygon_bunker helper + TestMultiPolygonBunkerAssignment (6 tests): RED->GREEN (4/6 fail on the old dispatch, builder-verified by stashing the fix) hole-9 assignment + corridor-cap + geometry-preserved-on-emit; multi-member decoy stays on hole 9; degenerate all-unusable -> drop; regression pin (existing osm_id assignments equal with/without the multipolygon appended). REVIEWER (fresh, adversarial) hand-traced the Red-9 geometry: main member straddles hole-9 centerline (~89m overlap) vs hole-8 0 overlap -> Tier 1 fires on hole 9; largest-member picks the main body (16x area vs decoy); centroid ~on line so dist ~0 << 150m cap; no Polygon/Point regression (per-polygon independent iterations); one osm_id -> one hole (no cross-hole spam); _ring_area closing-vertex trim identical to _ring_centroid. SHIP with 2 non-blocking test-strength notes (decoy places main at index 0 so doesn't isolate largest-wins; regression pin tautological-by-construction) — neither masks a defect. GATES: ruff clean; pytest test_course_spatial+test_osm_parsing 167/167; broader non-DB sweep 2534/2534; DB-backed ingest tests (test_ingest_osm_course, integration/) gated to CI's postgis backend gate on the pushed head. NOT shipped/pinged this cycle (SILENT; owner decisions pending in main session). VISIBLE PAYOFF: filed follow-up red9-relation-bunker-rerun (Red-only staging re-ingest, owner standing auth) writes the complex into stored features so it renders.

items.title: Verify relation 19545022 (claimed Red-9 waste complex) hole assignment — RESOLVED: correctly hole 11, premise falsified
items.why: Re-filed 2026-07-17 (had fallen out of backlog.json in the corruption-restore incident). Original concern (from the tree-distance verification era): OSM relation 19545022 — assembled by the course_spatial.py MultiPolygon branch (red9-relation-bunker-assembly) — was believed to be Bethpage Red hole 9's 'giant left-side waste complex' but landed on HOLE 11, feared as a hole-9 caddie blind-spot on the owner's weekend course + a phantom hazard on hole 11. Task: diagnose with real geometry, fix the assignment root cause, re-run Red's affected rows.
items.resolution: RESOLVED — NOT A BUG (premise falsified by three independent evidence lines); NO code change to the assignment heuristic, NO prod data write (either would REGRESS correct data). GEOMETRIC TRUTH: relation 19545022 is a single ~20x19 m greenside bunker (one outer ring + a 5 m island inner ring), OSM version 1, created 2025-08-31, NEVER edited — it was never a large sprawling waste complex. Centroid (lon -73.45612, lat 40.75158) is 14.0 m from Bethpage Red hole 11's centerline and 256.5 m from hole 9's. No hole centerline passes THROUGH it (Tier-1 overlap = 0 for every hole), so assign_features_to_holes decides it via Tier-2 ring-vertex voting -> nearest hole = 11 (6.7 m). EVIDENCE: (1) authoritative Overpass fetch of the relation (v1, 2-member multipolygon, 28 m extent); (2) offline reproduction with the real Red geometry — diag_red9_waste.py + diag_red_bunkers.py (backend/scripts/, live-Overpass, no DB): hole 9 ALREADY carries its own 7-bunker cluster (ways 1240889735/736/737/738/749/750/751, 14-53 m from its centerline) — the owner's 'giant left-side' area, surfaced by the v1.1.9 BUNKER_CAP 4->6 + fairway-adjacency raise, NOT by this relation; (3) read-only prod DB probe via key-free SSM (i-0826ae70df62d9fe8, DATABASE_URL read from the running process environ, never echoed): relation/19545022 stored under Bethpage Red HOLE 11 (40.75158,-73.45612) — correct; hole 9 has its 7 bunkers. ORIGIN OF THE MISBELIEF: red9-relation-bunker-assembly's why-note assumed the relation 'lands on hole 9 like a way-bunker'; that assumption was never geometry-checked. Bundle #143's own ship note already (correctly) called it 'the hole-11 waste-complex relation'. This item reconciles the two: it is hole 11's. BEFORE == AFTER (no write): hole 9 = 7 bunkers, hole 11 = relation/19545022 (+ its own), both correct. LANDED on integration/next (bundle #147, SILENT): a real-geometry regression pin TestRed9WasteComplexRealGeometry (backend/tests/test_course_spatial.py, 3 tests) locking the true hole-11 assignment (near hole wins, far hole 9 never does) so a future well-meaning 'fix' can't re-route it; + a clarifying note on the pre-existing synthetic TestMultiPolygonBunkerAssignment (its 'hole 9' is a fixture label, not the real relation's position); + the two diagnostic scripts. Gates: test_course_spatial 116/116, ruff clean.

items.title: Yardage plates (200/150/100) balloon at close zoom — use a fixed screen-space dot, not a ground-radius Circle
items.why: Flagged during the lettered-bunker-legend lane (owner v1.1.8 screenshot item-4 check). The 200/150/100 plates render in GoogleSatelliteMap.tsx addTeeShotOverlays as native Circles with radius:4 METERS (ground-anchored), so they visually BALLOON in screen px at close zoom — the 'large plain circles' the owner's screenshot showed. NOT a zoom artifact of the letter change and NOT a new regression: it is the shipped plate implementation's inherent behavior (fixed ground radius). VERDICT (designer + fable both): a distance plate marks a discrete yardage STATION, not a real-world-sized object — it should read as a fixed small SCREEN-space dot regardless of zoom (icon-marker idiom, like the bunker/tee glyphs, ~10-12px), not a fixed-meter circle. This is a Circle→icon MECHANISM swap: needs a plate glyph asset (or per-yardage colored dot PNGs matching PLATE_FILL_BY_YARDS), rework of the circles block + its id-tracking/removal, and re-verification that plate placement/visibility math is untouched. Medium effort, deserves its own fable plan + review. Deliberately deferred out of the letter lane to keep that change scoped. FILES: frontend/src/components/GoogleSatelliteMap.tsx (addTeeShotOverlays circles block, PLATE_FILL_BY_YARDS, teeShotCircleIdsRef).

Swap main.py:62 _owner_only -> _member on all 17 routers; keep require_owner on the by-nature owner-only endpoints (caller-voice/rehearsal-call, tee_times.py:633/767/787). (2) Close the gaps the flip exposes: [BLOCKING, reviewer pass] carve courses_mapped.py POST/PUT/DELETE (:68/:109/:126 — member-writable GLOBAL course geometry, no owner column; PUT/DELETE re-precompute elevations+guides so a stranger corrupts every user's caddie yardages) to STAY require_owner post-flip + negative write-authz test; hole_pins -> per-user (add user_id, unique key (course,hole,date,user_id), scope pins.py list/upsert — recommended, protects caddie yardage integrity; owner decision #6); stamp+match user_id on the _availability_jobs dict + any future async course-search job (uuid is not authz; NOTE scorecard /scan is synchronous, no job-id — and request_availability_call must stay owner-only OR require the member's own callbackNumber, never fall back to the owner's number); caddie_personas read=is_builtin|is_public|author==me, write/delete author-scoped 404. items.title: P0 hardening: clear scorecard_last_user_id + current namespace on Clerk signOut (pre-open-flip)
items.why: security-review LOW finding on multiuser-p0-client-identity (2026-07-16): nothing clears scorecard_last_user_id on sign-out, so on native (where window.Clerk.user.id often never hydrates) a same-device account switch can transiently resolve pref-store reads (persona/favorites/map-view) to the PRIOR user's namespace for one render before IdentityBridge's effect reconciles. items.title: P0: native JWT -> iOS Keychain (drop plaintext Capacitor Preferences token)
items.why: specs/multi-user-epic-plan.md §3.4 — App-Store-grade token-at-rest. lib/native-token-store.ts persists the Clerk session JWT PLAINTEXT in Capacitor Preferences (flagged in-file :12-15). Swap the backend for a Keychain-backed secure-storage plugin (kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly — encrypted at rest, excluded from iCloud/device backups); the file was written for a one-file swap. Include a one-time migrate-then-delete of the plaintext Preferences value. New iOS dependency (justify per CLAUDE.md) — verify the Capacitor webview Clerk login (native onBeforeRequest/onAfterResponse hooks) still round-trips the token on device. Security hardening; independent of the flip (can land anytime). /security-review (token handling).
items.done_note: DONE 2026-07-16 @59e899b on integration/next (SILENT/dark, bundle #144). Confirmed one-file swap: native-token-store.ts is the only toucher of @capacitor/preferences for key __clerk_client_jwt; callers (AuthProvider, ClerkTokenBridge) only import getNativeToken/setNativeToken/clearNativeToken. Swapped backend to @aparajita/capacitor-secure-storage@8.0.0 (iOS Keychain). Accessibility set EXPLICITLY to KeychainAccess.whenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly + sync:false on EVERY get/set/remove (plugin class-default is the weaker whenUnlocked / sync false — not relied on; no setSynchronize/setDefaultKeychainAccess mutation anywhere), so the JWT is encrypted at rest, excluded from iCloud/device backups, non-synchronizable. Race-safe migrate-then-delete: module-scoped in-flight migration promise dedups concurrent getNativeToken; writes Keychain FIRST then deletes plaintext (crash-between leaves token in both stores, never lost; idempotent re-migrate). clearNativeToken removes Keychain item AND any lingering plaintext prefs entry (zero residue either store on sign-out). Fails closed to null (re-auth) if Keychain unavailable; plaintext never deleted before a successful Keychain write. Web/non-native byte-identical (store native-only, callers gate). 8 new mocked unit tests (migrate+delete, no-resurrection, 3-way concurrent race, dual-store clear) 8/8. Reviewer (fresh, /security-review+/code-review scoped to the commit): SHIP — all 8 attack vectors (plaintext residue, accessibility/non-sync, sign-out clear, race/idempotency, downgrade/mixed-state, session-UX, dependency/lockfile provenance, Package.swift) attacked & SAFE; 3 LOW non-blocking follow-ups (reorder clear so prefs-remove runs even if keychain-remove throws; convertDate=false marginally cleaner; device-level backup-exclusion acceptance still owed pre-App-Store). QA PASS: lint/tsc/build/voice-278/vitest-2619/native-token-store-8/caddie-experience-241/ruff/npm-ci-dry-run all green + BONUS real iOS-sim (Xcode 26.4.1, iPhone 17) cap-sync + xcodebuild BUILD SUCCEEDED + launch + simctl-terminate cold-relaunch: no crash, session state persisted, keychain access event observed. REMAINING device-gap (not a defect): fresh-sign-in migrate-from-plaintext-scratch path is unit-tested (mocked) + code-inspected only; a real device/sim run with a seeded plaintext token + live Clerk OTP sign-in (XCUITest) is the final pre-App-Store acceptance, owed with the open-mode flip. Lockfile updated in place (npm 10): only @aparajita/capacitor-secure-storage@8.0.0 + its legit transitive deps (@capacitor/android, @capacitor/keyboard) added; net-reduction is a benign dedup of nested utf-8-validate, no platform bindings pruned; npm ci --dry-run consistent. This is the FINAL ready P0 foundation slice — P0 CODE COMPLETE (see multiuser-epic tracker note).

items.title: P2: Virtual Match MVP — async net match-play round-vs-round with live standings
items.why: specs/multi-user-epic-plan.md §5 (+ PM MVP shape) — the owner's headline ask. BLOCKED on P1 (needs the connection graph). MVP = two CONNECTED users each play their OWN round (existing scoring untouched) on a locked course/tee; a virtual_matches overlay computes NET match-play with a live/near-live standings feed. GROUNDING: computeMatchPlay (games.ts:739) is GROSS-only today — net allocation via HoleInfo.handicap is GENUINELY NEW logic (new pure lib/virtual-match.ts + a Python backend twin with parity tests vs games.ts fixtures, since the client can't read the opponent's raw round). Schema: virtual_matches (creator/opponent, status, per-side round_ids, locked course/tee, frozen_handicaps snapshot, result, expires_at) + optional Round.virtualMatchId mirroring tournamentId. State machine proposed->accepted->active->completed (+declined/cancelled/expired); consent structural (nothing visible pre-accept; attach-only-own-round); expiry void unless zero-hole no-show (owner decision #11). ANTI-TAMPER: neither can touch the other's scores (falls out of P0); opponent sees a minimized scores-only projection (never GPS/caddie/memories). Client: 'Challenge' on a Partner profile + quiet match card + result screen (reuse leaderboard/SettleUp visual language, NOT a new tab); caddie answers 'how's my match with Mike' as DATA not prompt-trusted text. DEFERRED: simultaneous/live, groups/leagues, money settlement, chat, rematches. /security-review + designer NORTHSTAR review. Cycle count ~3-4.

items.title: Should a literal close->reopen of the converse orb sheet preserve conversation continuity?
items.why: Surfaced by the cycle-133 builder while writing the no-re-greet test: the designer concept assumed 'turns persist across close->reopen (safe today)' but that is FALSE — CaddieOrbSheet's setOpen((wasOpen)=>{ if(!wasOpen) resetSession(); return true; }) intentionally clears turns on every closed->open transition (pre-existing, unrelated to this cycle). So the converse orb starts a FRESH conversation on every reopen, whereas the round-side sheet preserves its thread. The builder correctly did NOT change this reset behavior (a real session-persistence change, out of scope for a copy-only nit) and re-scoped the test to the true invariant (continuity across a re-summon while the sheet STAYS open; the emptyHint never re-greets once turns.length>0). Reviewer + designer both flagged the underlying UX question as a fair product call for later. ACTION: owner decides whether converse close->reopen should preserve conversation continuity (matching round) or intentionally start fresh; if preserve, it's a small state-lifetime change to resetSession's trigger. UPDATE 2026-07-16: the IN-ROUND case is now SATISFIED by the untethered-caddie work (bundle #142, Item B) -- live-session ownership was lifted from CaddieSheet to RoundPageClient (useDetachedCaddieLive), so closing/reopening the round CaddieSheet preserves the running session + transcript. REMAINING for this item: only the off-course CONVERSE orb (CaddieOrbSheet.resetSession on closed->open) -- still needs-owner-decision whether it should preserve or start fresh.

items.title: Caddie flips its recommendation across identical asks (tee-shot lay-up vs stick with driver)
items.resolution: DONE on bundle #141 2026-07-16 (cycle 130, integration/next, NOTICEABLE -- advice consistency). Feature commit e3026b5. Mechanism = PAYLOAD-ANCHOR, not temperature (temp rejected: GA Realtime payload has no temp param so the realtime mouth can't be tuned, and run_consistency.py hard-codes its own temp=0.7 so a production temp change is invisible to the probe -- unfalsifiable). New shared DECISION_GROUNDING_RULE (backend/app/caddie/voice_prompts.py) extends the numbers-coherence doctrine from NUMBERS to the CALL across BOTH mouths (realtime instructions + both routes/caddie.py stable_text blocks): the engine's recommended club IS the call -- the caddie explains it and offers grounded comparisons of a floated club but does not freely re-decide; flips only when numbers favor the alternative or the player gives NEW info the engine lacked. items.title: run_latency p95 extrapolates above the observed max on small n
items.resolution: DONE on bundle #141 (cycle 134, 2026-07-16, integration/next @5fa725d, SILENT/tooling). Extracted a pure importable _p95(latencies_ms) in run_latency.py: statistics.quantiles(..., method='inclusive') (interpolates within [min,max], never extrapolates) wrapped in min(raw, max(latencies)) as a belt-and-suspenders clamp; n==1 -> that value, empty -> ValueError; JSON report gains p95_clamped_to_observed_max. run() calls _p95(); module stays import-safe (OPENAI/DB import deferred). New collected test_latency_quantile.py pins the n=10 2026-07-15 shape (p95 <= max, RED against the old exclusive formula which reviewer reproduced at 945>869ms), n==1, empty->ValueError, p95>=median. items.why: CADDIE-EXPERIENCE dim 7 harness-refinement, found by the 2026-07-15 baseline. run_latency.py computes p95 as statistics.quantiles(latencies_ms, n=20)[18] (default 'exclusive' method), which EXTRAPOLATES above the largest observed sample on small n -- with n=10 it reported p95=1138ms while the actual max sample was 869ms, i.e. a 'p95' higher than any real measurement. ACTION: for n<20 use method='inclusive' or clamp/report the observed max so the reported p95 never exceeds a measured value; add a tiny unit assertion (p95 <= max(samples)). items.title: Follow-up: complete the deferred money-settling game formats (wolf true-transfer, opponent/team pickers)
items.why: Deferred from cycle 100 (tournament-settlement-honesty, @2e17261 on integration/next / PR #135). That cycle made the picker HONEST by only offering a stake for formats that truly settle zero-sum (STAKE_GAME_IDS = {skins, match, nassau}) and PREVENTING silently-truncated games. Three capabilities were intentionally deferred as product/design-shaped, each a real feature the owner may want later: (1) WOLF as a money game -- computeWolf (games.ts ~836-862) is NOT zero-sum today: lone-mode credits the wolf +/-3 and debits no one; partner-mode awards +1/+1 to the winning pair and debits no one (reviewer falsified: a lone-wolf win netted +$6, sum != 0). To re-enable wolf stakes, make the deltas TRUE transfers (lone win: wolf +3, each other -1; lone loss: wolf -3, each other +1; partner: distribute the pot across the losing side -- a real rules decision) then re-add 'wolf' to SETTLEABLE_FORMATS + STAKE_GAME_IDS and add a decided-multi-hole zero-sum test (the new SETTLEABLE_FORMATS property test in settlement.test.ts will enforce it). (2) MATCH-PLAY opponent picker -- match play is restricted to exactly-2 rosters (prevents silent drop of players 3+); a >2 roster needs an opponent-selection UI writing game.settings.matchPlayPlayers (engine already supports it, games.ts:740-741). (3) TEAM-ASSIGNMENT UI -- unlocks stakes for the team formats currently non-settling-by-construction in the picker (vegas is team-only/unconstructible today; bestBall/scramble/threePoint need teams). Each is its own plan+tests; keep [[no-fake-data-fallbacks]]: never re-offer a stake until the format settles zero-sum end-to-end.
items.resolution: PART 1 (WOLF) SHIPPED TO MAIN 2026-07-12 (cycle 110, @741e6ac, bundle #137 merged @c715d2e; owner 'Ship it'; TestFlight v1.1.5 build 202607121234). Also in this bundle: threePoint zero-sum rounding-drift fix (cycle 112, @1ee8427, tournament settlement coverage hardening — real odd-cent-stake money bug). Option A per specs/tournament-wolf-settlement-plan.md (fable): computeWolf now emits a TRUE zero-sum per-hole pointsDelta (lone win wolf +3 / each of 3 opponents -1; lone loss mirror; partner win winners +1 / losers -1; partner loss mirror; ties and any incomplete-data hole -- missing pick, missing wolf score, or fewer than all 3 opponent scores in lone mode -- emit an EMPTY delta / $0), and settlement.ts settles money = totals x pointValue and re-adds 'wolf' to SETTLEABLE_FORMATS (STAKE_GAME_IDS derives it, so buildRoundGames carries wolf's stake again). items.title: Bethpage 7/11 guide-validation: gap-class carry-SPAN fix SHIPPED (cycle 116, v1.1.6); two residual false-reject sub-classes filed
items.why: INVESTIGATION 2026-07-09 (cycle 39, OFFLINE from committed fixture tests/fixtures/bethpage_overpass.json — same data prod ingested; no prod SSM needed). Holes 7 & 11 were persistently honest-empty (research ~4x each fails validate_guide). ROOT CAUSE = (c) validator over-strict, NOT (a) geometry or (b) OSM. EVIDENCE: (a) ruled out — extract_hole_hazards gives correct sides/carries matching reality: hole 7 par-5 dogleg-right (polyline 346->36->55deg) bunkers R 170/430/520 + L 355/525; hole 11 par-4 bunkers both sides L 245/415 R 270/325/420. All normal/correct non-numbered guide phrasings PASS the validator. (b) ruled out on-hole — 7/11 have ONLY bunkers, nearest water 395-431y OFF the played line (not in play); a water/OB mention is therefore a CORRECT rejection (OSM has no 'ob' polygon type so any OB phrase always rejects — expected). (c) CONFIRMED + reproduced: _side_and_carry_supported() checks a claimed carry vs each bunker's CENTROID +-25y, but Bethpage bunkers are large — hole 7's right cross-bunker centroid=170y actually SPANS 103-280y (178y wide). A web-researched guide naturally citing any carry in the real footprint (220/240/250/260) but >25y from centroid -> WHOLE guide REJECTED (demonstrated: all four reject). That is why grounded, reasonable numbered advice keeps failing = the persistence pattern. DECISION NEEDED (genuine product/risk call, not an obvious fix): (c1) widen the carry check to the bunker's surveyed near->far carry SPAN (recovers guides on big-bunker holes) — but this loosens the grounding/anti-laundering control that had a bypass closed just days ago (5e4b861 carry-tie-break laundering), so it REQUIRES the full adversarial treatment: Fable plan + eval-harness teeth (each new accept-case proven RED pre-fix) + adversarial review proving no false number laundering re-opens; vs (c2) accept status quo — honest-empty is SAFE and correct (caddie falls back to the grounded generic hazard line); large-bunker holes simply get no cached prose guide. Recommend (c1) as a properly-reviewed future build item IF cached guides on such holes are wanted; otherwise close as working-as-designed. Optional confirmation: an on-box research pass capturing the exact rejected text would pin which number/phrase fired in prod, but the local reproduction already demonstrates the mechanism — not worth an unattended prod SSM action. FILES: backend/app/caddie/guide_writer.py (_side_and_carry_supported, _has_side_flip, _CARRY_TOLERANCE_YARDS=25); backend/app/caddie/hazards.py (extract_hole_hazards, centroid-based carry). FORBIDDEN to inline-fix per tasks/lessons.md (geometry/validation change without full adversarial treatment).
items.resolution: CYCLE 116 (2026-07-12): the carry-SPAN / contiguous-run fix (option c1, properly reviewed) LANDED in v1.1.6 (commit 5e4414b, PR #139; specs/guide-validator-carry-span-plan.md fable plan; reviewer opus SHIP after CONSTRUCTING a fabricated carry [BLACK-7 R{170,430,520} 'right bunker at 300'] and proving it still REJECTS; qa PASS ruff+137/137 targeted+2365 broad offline; backend-only). _side_and_carry_supported now accepts a claimed (type,side,carry) within [min-25,max+25] of a CONTIGUOUS RUN of that (type,side)'s sampled carries (bunker/water/ob bridge _CARRY_BRIDGE_YARDS=60; trees bridge unconditionally into one [min,max] run since the near/far pair brackets a continuous line). Strict SUPERSET of old accepts -> ZERO existing-guide regressions (Black 16 / Red 15 / Pebble 18 intact). On-box regen (sanctioned; fixed code from 5e4414b materialized to /tmp, app .env, LOOPER_SECRETS_DISABLED=1, claude-sonnet-5, one bounded pass) of the 5 negative-cached holes: 3/5 RECOVERED -> RED 1/8/18 now cached & grounded (Red 18/18; e.g. Red 8 'left bunkers at 160 and 195'=geom {160,195,365}). BLACK 7/11 re-rejected -> honest-empty (Black still 16/18). The on-box diagnostic REVISED the cycle-39 footprint theory: (i) BLACK 11 = a DIFFERENT, pre-existing false-reject in _has_side_flip NUMBER-BINDING (untouched here) -- the both-sides sentence 'the 245-left bunker and the 270/325 right-side bunkers' binds right-side 325 to the nearby LEFT bunker keyword's window and rejects, though every number is correctly grounded to its true side; (ii) BLACK 7 = STOCHASTIC (a fresh diagnostic candidate PASSED; the regen candidate cited an ungrounded number) -- left honest-empty, no retry storm. The single-large-bunker-footprint case was NOT reproduced this pass (lower confidence than the cycle-39 write-up). Two follow-ups filed: guide-validator-cross-side-binding (the observed BLACK 11 cause) + guide-validator-bunker-footprint-span (the cycle-39 theory). Honest-empty stays SAFE (generic grounded hazard line). CYCLE 118 (2026-07-13): the cross-SIDE binding sub-class is FIXED + proven (commit 12cec56, see guide-validator-cross-side-binding=done; a fresh real Black-11 both-sides candidate now VALIDATES PASS on prod geometry). Black 7/11 still honest-empty (Black 16/18) because a THIRD residual surfaced on-box -- cross-TYPE number pollution (a trees carry adjacent to a bunker keyword, validated vs bunker geometry) filed as guide-validator-cross-type-number-binding -- plus Black-7 play_line verbosity (397>240). One of the two cycle-116 sub-classes now closed; two residuals (footprint-span [low-confidence], cross-type-number-binding [observed]) remain.

items.title: Swap native JWT storage backend from UserDefaults to Keychain plugin
items.rationale: Final part of clerk-jwt-keychain. Replace @capacitor/preferences (UserDefaults, plaintext, backup-exposed) with @aparajita/capacitor-secure-storage using kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly so the Clerk JWT is encrypted at rest + excluded from device/iCloud backups. Thanks to native-token-store.ts this is a single-file backend swap (+ add the plugin + cap sync). NOTE: adds a native dependency (SPM/pod) and the actual Keychain persistence can only be fully verified on a real device/sim sign-in -> bundle with other device-verifiable changes or verify via a credentialed sim run before shipping. REQUIRED before App Store / wider release; NOT beta-blocking (owner-only TestFlight).
review_followups: LOW (TOCTOU, reviewer 2026-06-28): an in-flight FAPI response can re-persist the JWT just after clearNativeToken() on sign-out. Security-nil (Clerk destroys the session server-side; re-persisted token is revoked). Fix when doing the Keychain swap: gate setNativeToken on a module-level 'signed out' flag that clearNativeToken sets and a sign-in transition (false->true) resets — taking care NOT to suppress the re-sign-in token write (it happens during the sign_in FAPI exchange, before isSignedIn flips).

LOW (cold-start stale token, reviewer 2026-06-28): if sign-out happens while the app is killed, the stale (already server-invalid) JWT isn't cleared until it's injected and rejected. Fix: in nativeOnAfterResponse, clearNativeToken() on a FAPI authentication-failure error code — but first confirm the EXACT Clerk FAPI error code via a credentialed run so a valid token is never wrongly wiped.

items.title: EPIC: Map/GPS + caddie intelligence (plays-like, DECADE-style management)

items.title: Harden guide_writer pause_turn continuation re-serialization (low-pri)
items.resolution: LANDED on integration/next (fresh bundle PR #141) cycle 125 (2026-07-15), SILENT. Fable plan specs/guide-pauseturn-reserialize-hardening-plan.md (SDK-verified vs anthropic 0.77.0 / pydantic 2.12.5). Commit 941cf96: guide_writer.py pause_turn continuation now re-sends result.content (SDK block objects) directly instead of [c.model_dump(mode='json') for c in result.content] — Anthropic's documented continuation pattern. Root cause confirmed empirically: a server-tool block outside the non-beta ContentBlock union (e.g. bash_code_execution_tool_result) deserializes via construct_type to a TextBlock with text=None; re-dumping emits a corrupted wire dict ({'citations':None,'text':None,'type':'bash_code_execution_tool_result',...}); the SDK send-path serializes the same objects losslessly (exclude_unset), and messages.parse forwards messages through the identical transform body as .create — so .parse needs nothing different. Tests: 2 new offline tests in test_guide_writer.py using real SDK types; RED->GREEN proven (identity assertion 'content is paused_content' + non-dict fails under old code). reviewer(fresh) SHIP (reproduced old-code corruption + confirmed lossless send-path + both tests RED-under-old/GREEN-under-new; no regression to non-pause path; no security surface; one non-blocking nitpick: assertion (b) env-dependent/redundant, identity assertion is load-bearing). qa PASS (ruff clean; pytest test_guide_writer.py 114/114 incl. both new; wider guide/caddie sweep 242 passed / 22 pre-existing DB-skip). No migration, no shared-types change, no DB surface. Awaiting the next noticeable change to ride the bundle to owner.
items.why: Prod smoke (2026-07-11) noted: guide_writer.py:278 re-serializes assistant content via model_dump(mode='json') in the pause_turn continuation loop; when the model returns code_execution/text_editor_code_execution server-tool blocks (not web_search), pydantic emits PydanticSerializationUnexpectedValue warnings. NON-FATAL (the smoke hole finished first-turn), but a hole needing a real pause_turn continuation with those block types may not re-serialize cleanly. Fix: resume with the SDK block objects directly rather than a manual model_dump. Low priority.

items.landed: 2026-07-16 on integration/next (bundle #143, NOTICEABLE). Plan specs/oncourse-gps-target-origin-plan.md. Wired the existing tapTargetDistances origin+fromGps plumbing to the live GPS: extracted isGpsPlausibleToGreen (5-800y, shared with RoundPageClient posOnHole byte-identical), resolveGpsOrigin feeds tapTargetForPos + placeTarget leg-1 so tap AND drag anchor at the golfer when on-hole (label 'From you' per designer, else 'From tee'); live re-place on GPS ticks via a createSerialRunner FIFO mutex (all placeTarget/clearTapMarker callers serialized — closes a reviewer-caught orphan race, v1.1.9 class). Amber TO-GREEN leg unchanged. Verdicts: reviewer SHIP (mutex traced+falsified), qa PASS (lint/tsc/build/voice 278/vitest 773), designer APPROVE-WITH-COPY. Gates green on the landed head.
items.why: specs/oncourse-gps-readiness-plan.md (owner's Bethpage Red readiness pass, 2026-07-16), behavior #8 of the GPS-consumer audit: GoogleSatelliteMap.tsx's tap-to-measure handler (tap 782-824) hardcodes the origin at the tee (fromGps=false) even when the golfer's live GPS position is known and on-hole — the FROM number and the drawn line should originate at the player, not the tee, in that case. The distance-from-point helper already supports an arbitrary origin; this is a bounded follow-up (P2) confined to GoogleSatelliteMap.tsx, a map-lane hot file (fix/map-fieldtest-v119) — deliberately NOT touched by this harness pass to avoid conflict.

items.title: elevation.py int(ref) crashes on composite hole refs like '1 - #2' (Pinehurst format)
items.landed: 2026-07-17 on integration/next (bundle #146, SILENT). Added app.services.course_spatial.parse_leading_int_ref(ref) -> Optional[int]: takes only the leading run of digits at the START of the (trimmed) string, so '7'->7, '1 - #2'->1, '12A'->12, '#3'/junk/None->None (never a fake 0). elevation.py's sample_course_elevations now uses it (was a bare int(ref) crash); course_spatial.build_course_feature_collection now uses it too and SKIPS refs that don't parse instead of emitting a 'hole 0' via the old _ref_to_int fallback (that fallback fn + its 3 existing tests are left untouched/unused in prod — avoided touching a test that encodes its old default-to-0 contract). Tests: TestParseLeadingIntRef (9 cases) in test_course_spatial.py, TestBuildCourseFeatureCollectionCompositeRef (3 cases), TestSampleCourseElevationsCompositeRef (3 cases, mocked fetch_3dep_samples) in test_hole_elevation_ingest.py.
items.why: Found during the 2026-07-17 championship-course ingest op: backend/app/services/elevation.py:448 int(ref) raises on OSM refs like '1 - #2', and course_spatial._ref_to_int returns 0 for the same, collapsing hole numbers. Worked around at runtime with a leading-int normalization wrapper; needs the real repo fix (shared leading-int ref parser) + tests covering composite/dash/hash ref formats.

items.title: Same-boundary duplicate hole refs (e.g. Pine Valley short course) contaminate par merge + polygon grouping
items.landed: 2026-07-17 on integration/next (bundle #146, SILENT). Added course_spatial._linestring_length_m(coords) (sums _deg_to_m over consecutive vertices). osm_ingest.apply_boundary_hole_selection now dedupes by ref among the inside-the-boundary candidate set BEFORE tagging: when >1 candidate hole shares a ref, only the longest way is tagged with target_course_name; the shorter duplicate(s) are left untouched (as if outside the boundary), so par merge/polygon grouping downstream never sees the collision. Refless holes are exempt (can't collide). Tests: TestDuplicateHoleRefDedupe (5 cases: longest-wins, loser-untagged, non-colliding-refs-unaffected, 3-way collision, refless-never-collide) in test_osm_boundary_selection.py, plus a full apply_boundary_hole_selection->assemble_osm_course integration regression (TestBoundarySelectionDuplicateRefIntegration, wired exactly as scripts/ingest_osm_course.py does) in test_ingest_osm_course.py proving hole 1's par stays 4 (main course), never contaminated to 3 (short course).
items.why: Found 2026-07-17: when a club boundary contains two courses whose holes share refs (Pine Valley main + short course), par merge and polygon grouping mix them (first ingest produced holes 1-10 all par 3; caught by audit). Runtime fix was a longest-way-per-ref dedupe wrapper; needs the repo fix in the ingest path + a regression test with a two-course-one-boundary fixture.

items.title: fetch_golf_course_boundaries swallows 429/504 into an empty list (reads as 'no boundary matched')
items.landed: 2026-07-17 on integration/next (bundle #147, SILENT). Eng-lead decided the contract: added app.services.osm.OverpassThrottledError; _post_with_retry gained an opt-in raise_on_failure=True param (default False, all existing callers unchanged) that raises OverpassThrottledError instead of returning None when a transient failure (429/5xx, or timeout/transport error) exhausts the retry budget. fetch_golf_course_boundaries is the only caller that opts in — a non-transient 4xx (e.g. 400/406) still returns None -> [] (query-shape bug, not throttling, left out of scope). Call site: scripts/ingest_osm_course.py's --boundary-name path (the only real caller) now try/excepts OverpassThrottledError around the fetch and exits 1 with a message explicit that this is retryable, not a genuine 'no boundary matched' -- no other in-process multi-course driver exists (each course is ingested via its own process invocation). Renamed test_overpass_failure_returns_empty_list -> {test_504_exhausted_raises_throttled_error, test_429_exhausted_raises_throttled_error, test_timeout_exhausted_raises_throttled_error, test_true_empty_result_returns_empty_list} in test_osm_boundary_selection.py -- sanctioned contract change per eng-lead decision, not a weakened assertion (throttle now raises; true empty still returns []). ruff clean; 45/45 targeted tests + full offline tests/ suite (2814 passed) green.
items.why: Found 2026-07-17 under heavy Overpass throttling: HTTP 429/504 from Overpass is caught and returned as [], indistinguishable from a genuine no-boundary result, sending the ingest down misleading failure paths. Raise/annotate throttle errors distinctly (retry-able) vs true empties, + tests. DEFERRED 2026-07-17 (builder, same pass as the two p2 fixes above): making fetch_golf_course_boundaries distinguish throttle-failure from genuine-empty means changing its on-failure return contract, which directly conflicts with the existing test_osm_boundary_selection.py::test_overpass_failure_returns_empty_list assertion (504 x2 exhausted -> must equal []). That's a real design call (new sibling fn returning a tri-state result? change the test's expectation deliberately? raise a typed exception only some callers catch?) rather than a 'stays small' tweak, so left untouched rather than force a fix that breaks a test encoding the current contract. Needs an eng-lead call on the target shape before a builder should touch this.

ui_decision_social_course: OWNER OVERRIDE (2026-06-28, IMG_2960): build a floating 'island' pill bottom tab bar (Instagram-style — rounded, hovers above the bottom edge, NOT edge-to-edge SaaS) for navigating the future features. This supersedes the earlier 'no bottom tab' recommendation. Build it in the yardage-book aesthetic (on-paper, restrained, calm — not generic SaaS) and HIDE it on immersive screens (active round / voice / GPS) where the app should disappear. Likely tabs: Round/Home · Partners · Courses (+ profile). This is the 'minimal home dock' path from the plan, now promoted to the chosen approach + extended to a persistent floating tab. A new card 'nav-floating-island-tab' tracks the build.
